race.name: L'Etape Costa Rica by Tour de France
race.display_name: L'Etape Costa Rica by Tour de France
race.tagline: 130km of volcanic fury near Irazú—Tour de France spirit ignites Central America's savage climbs!

vitals.field_size: Hundreds expected in Central America's Tour de France amateur showdown
vitals.registration: Pre-registration locks lowest fees until Sept 2025; includes official jersey and Tour-level access
vitals.prize_purse: No cash prizes; glory via polka-dot and green jersey competitions
vitals.aid_stations: Multiple food/drink stations mirroring Tour de France support
vitals.cutoff_time: TBD; self-paced amateur challenge with flexible finishes
distance_options: Ride: ~52km, ~1,700ft — gentler intro to volcanic vibes

climate.primary: Tropical highland — humid heat with afternoon storms
climate.description: August brings warm days around 75-85°F in Cartago's highlands, laced with high humidity from nearby rainforests. Expect morning clarity giving way to potential showers near Irazú. Riders battle sweat-soaked suffering amid lush volcanic backdrops.

terrain.primary: Volcanic paved climbs with Tour pedigree
terrain.surface: Smooth Costa Rican pavement snakes through lush highlands, freshly tarmacked in key sectors for pro-level speed. Expect grippy asphalt hugging Irazú's flanks, with minimal gravel threats. Road closures ensure pure, traffic-free flow.

gravel_god_rating.tier_note: T2 fits this nascent Tour de France import—prestige punches above its rookie weight, but unproven field depth and logistics cap the ceiling. Volcanic adventure screams potential for T1 glory once it matures. Central America's first L'Etape demands respect, not dominance yet.

logistics.explanation: Cartago's remote but accessible; SJO airport works, but expect shuttle scrambles. Lodging's basic—grab a highland finca early. Tour Series polish shines, yet Costa Rican chaos lurks; solid for adventurers, frustrating for divas.

length.explanation: 130km hits sportive sweet spot—long enough to shatter souls, short enough for mortals. Race option crushes with volcanic heft. Perfect for weekend warriors eyeing Tour glory without multi-day madness.

technicality.explanation: Paved perfection, no gravel hell, but hairpin descents demand focus amid sweat-fogged glasses. Mid-tier tech suits roadies; climbs test legs over bike-handling wizardry. Reliable, not revolutionary.

elevation.explanation: 2,500m near Irazú? Brutal starter punch for tropics. Repeated kicks mimic Tour suffering without Alps insanity. Elite enough to badge-drop, accessible for strong amateurs.

climate.explanation: Humid August furnace tests mental steel—sweat rivers, pop-up storms. Highland breeze saves descents, but heat saps watts. Pack arm warmers; weather's wild card swings epic or epic fail.

altitude.explanation: Irazú's 11,000ft tease hits 3,000m+ peaks—real hypoxia bite for sea-level slugs. Acclimatize or crumble; elevates this from fun ride to legit sufferfest. Tropical high-alt magic.

adventure.explanation: Volcanic jungles, Tour branding, Central American edge—pure escapism. Irazú's foggy rims and rainforest roars scream bucket-list. This ain't France; it's wilder, weirder, way more alive.

prestige.explanation: Official L'Etape pedigree catapults it skyward—polka-dot glory awaits. New kid, but Tour de France halo blinds doubters. Finisher's jersey flexes harder than most gran fondos.

race_quality.explanation: Series pros deliver closed roads, aid stations, timing—Tour replica on budget. Routes pending, but volcano promise screams quality. Expect polish with Costa Rican flair.

experience.explanation: Amateurs become pros amid Irazú's drama—jerseys, crowds, volcanic payoff. Unforgettable immersion; L'Etape magic transplants perfectly to tropics. Life-changer disguised as bike ride.

community.explanation: Growing Central Am scene plus global Series draw; locals hungry, internationals elite. Not yet Unbound-deep, but Tour buzz builds fast. Solid vibes, room to explode.

field_depth.explanation: Rookie event—no battle-tested peloton yet. Expect mixed bags from tourists to ticos; depth builds post-2025 debut. Potential pro-am clashes, current thin ranks.

value.explanation: Pre-reg steals at sub-$250; jersey, support, prestige crush ROI. Beats Euro flights for similar suffering. Bargain Tour taste in paradise.

expenses.explanation: Flights to SJO cheapish, Cartago lodging affordable, but import bike fees sting. Event fee fair, travel adds up for gringos. Value trumps cost for adventurers.

biased_opinion.summary: L'Etape Costa Rica blasts Tour de France fever into Irazú's fiery jaws—rookie guts with pro polish. 130km of paved pain amid jungle drama crushes souls while jerseys crown kings. Central America's answer to Alps envy: cheaper, wilder, sweatier. Debut vibes scream breakout star; skip if you fear humidity hell.
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Premier Central Am gran fondo with Tour DNA—jump in early for founding glory. Prestige elevates it beyond regional rivals. Bucket-list no-brainer for climb chasers.

final_verdict.one_liner: Tour spirit erupts on Irazú—rookie rocket with global glow
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you crave Tour jerseys amid volcanoes and tropical chaos—perfect for adventure roadies. Skip if humidity horrors or logistics laziness plague you; wait for 2027 maturity.
final_verdict.alternatives: L'Etape Slovenia for Euro climbs (letape-slovenia); La Ruta for MTB brutality (la-ruta); Costa Rica gravel fests for dirt twists

logistics.airport: Fly into SJO (Juan Santamaría, 45min north); shuttle or rent car to Cartago. Domestic SJO-Cartago flights rare—drive the Pan-Am for scenery.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Book Cartago fincas or hotels early; aim highland spots near start. Airbnb gems abound—volcano views beat sterile chains.
logistics.food: Tico gallo pinto fuels mornings; post-ride casados and imperials await. Event aid mirrors Tour; hydrate against humidity.
logistics.packet_pickup: Likely Cartago expo day prior; Tour Series efficiency expected.
logistics.parking: Ample near start with road closures; carpool advised.

history.founder: L'Étape Series by Tour de France organizers
history.origin_story: Born 2023 to export Tour de France amateur magic to Central America. Targets Costa Rica's volcanic wilds near Irazú for ultimate sufferfest. Debuted massively with 2025 edition, cementing as region's premier cycling showdown. Pre-2026 hype exploded post-launch.
notable_moments: Irazú routes teased for pro-am pain
history.reputation: Instant buzz as Tour's tropical outpost—prestige-soaked rookie drawing global eyes. Riders rave volcanic beauty meets Series pro-standards. Poised for iconic status.

course_description.character: Paved volcanic rollercoaster from Cartago shadows Irazú's brooding mass—steep ramps pierce cloud forests, descents carve lush canyons. Race route doubles down on climbs for Tour-true brutality; Ride eases in. Closed roads let amateurs chase polka-dots amid jungle roars.

suffering_zones.label: Irazú Foothills
suffering_zones.desc: First real leg-breaker ramps up through steaming highlands. Gradient bites early, humidity magnifies pain. Tour-style markers hype the polka-dot hunt.

suffering_zones.label: Volcano Vista KOM
suffering_zones.desc: Peak suffering near Irazú's foggy rim—sustained grind to thin air. Legs scream, views stun; signature descent rockets to valley.

suffering_zones.label: Cartago Crusher
suffering_zones.desc: Final haul back to town tests cracked resolve. Short but savage, crowds fuel finish surge.

course_description.signature_challenge: Irazú Volcano ascent—2,500m cumulative near 11,000ft peak redefines tropical climbing. Pavement belies the hypoxic punch; foggy rims and jungle heat forge Tour legends from amateurs.

guide_variables.race_name: L'Etape Costa Rica by Tour de France

non_negotiables.why: Irazú's cumulative 2,500m demands vert legs; tropics amplify fatigue. Build heat tolerance too—untrained hit the wall early. Tour prestige requires readiness.
Highland altitude sneaks up; pre-adapt or bonk spectacularly. Test gear in humidity. Mental prep for solo suffering zones.
